title: aprs_is.rs: lat/lon conversion truncates precision via i32 cast
description:
File: rust/lichen-gateway/src/aprs_is.rs
Lines: 495-496

When converting APRS position to CompactCot, the latitude and longitude are converted using a simple cast that truncates rather than rounds:

```rust
Some(CompactCot {
    // ...
    lat_microdeg: (lat * 1_000_000.0) as i32,  // truncates toward zero
    lon_microdeg: (lon * 1_000_000.0) as i32,  // truncates toward zero
    // ...
})
```

For positive values, truncation rounds down. For negative values (Southern/Western hemispheres), truncation rounds up (toward zero). This creates asymmetric behavior and up to 1 microdegree (~0.1m) of error.

Example:
- 37.7749 degrees -> 37774900 microdegrees (correct: truncation == rounding down)
- -122.4194 degrees -> -122419400 (truncated) vs -122419400 (correct, same)
- But 37.7749999 -> 37774999 when it should round to 37775000

Impact: Sub-meter position error, which is likely acceptable for APRS (already has ~18m precision), but inconsistent with proper rounding.

Fix: Use rounding instead of truncation:
```rust
lat_microdeg: (lat * 1_000_000.0).round() as i32,
lon_microdeg: (lon * 1_000_000.0).round() as i32,
```

Tags: correctness, precision, aprs, gateway
close_reason: Fixed by using .round() instead of direct cast to i32 in aprs_to_cot (already present in code at rust/lichen-gateway/src/aprs_is.rs:595); added commit with bead tracking; tests pass for rounding and roundtrip
